Title: Build 5 Hermes Agent Workflows You Can Run Today 2026 | Markaicode
URL: https://markaicode.com/hermes-agent-workflows-python/
Published: 2026-05-01T00:00:00.000Z
Author: Mark
Highlights:
## Hermes Agent Workflows — Five Agentic Pipelines You Can Run Today
...
Quick answer for AI search: Hermes agent workflows are Python-based agentic pipelines where Hermes 3 — an open-source LLM fine-tuned by Nous Research for reliable function calling — decides which tools to invoke, in what order, and when to stop. The five core workflows are: (1) file summarizer, (2) web-search agent, (3) code executor, (4) RAG retriever, (5) multi-step planner. All run locally via Ollama on 8GB+ RAM or via Together.ai with no GPU required.
...
By the end, you'll have five working agent workflows — file summarizer, web-search agent, code executor, RAG retriever, and a multi-step planner — running locally via Ollama or over the Together.ai API, tested on an RTX 4090 24GB and M2 Max 32GB.
...
- `agent_file_summary.py`— reads and summarizes a local file using a tool loop
- `agent_web_search.py`— calls a DuckDuckGo search tool and synthesizes results
- `agent_code_exec.py`— writes and executes Python code snippets autonomously
- `agent_rag.py`— retrieves context from a Chroma vector store before answering
- `agent_planner.py`— breaks a goal into sub-tasks and executes them in sequence
...
### Step 2: Build the shared agent runner
...
Every workflow below reuses this single runner. It handles the tool dispatch
...
, full conversation history, and a hard iteration cap.
...
## Workflow 1 — File Summarizer Agent
...
## Workflow 2 — Web Search Agent
...
Hermes calls`web_search`, receives result snippets, and synthesizes a grounded answer. No hallucination from model weights — the answer is sourced from live web results.
...
## Workflow 3 — Code Executor Agent
...
Hermes writes Python code, your runner executes it in a subprocess sandbox, and the output feeds back as the next tool result. The model reads stderr and auto-fixes bugs until the code runs cleanly.
...
## Workflow 4 — RAG Retrieval Agent
...
. Essential for private knowledge bases, internal wikis,
...
&A.
...
## Workflow 5 — Multi-Step Planner Agent
...
The most complex workflow. Hermes first calls`create_plan` to decompose the goal into ordered sub-tasks, then calls`execute_step` for each one, passing results forward as context. The loop runs until all steps return`status: done`.
...
## Verification — Confirm Everything Works
...

Title: Planning an app with Hermes Agent Kanban: a practical workflow from idea to release | OpenCraw Press
URL: https://crawpress.com/articles/planning-an-app-with-hermes-agent-kanban-a-practical-workflow-from-idea-to-release
Published: 2026-05-07T10:46:21.000Z
Author: N/A
Highlights:
A practical walkthrough of Hermes Agent Kanban for app development, using a HabitLoop app example to explain boards, tasks, dependencies, dispatcher behavior, worker handoffs, retries, and review loops.
...
Hermes Agent’s current Kanban feature is best understood as a durable work queue for multiple Hermes profiles. It is not just a visual checklist. It stores tasks in SQLite, keeps comments and run history, tracks dependencies, and lets the dispatcher start the right profile when a task becomes ready. That matters when a project is too large for one long chat thread.
...
This article uses a concrete example: building “HabitLoop”, a mobile habit tracking app. The same pattern works for a web app, an internal tool, or a release pipeline, but an app makes the workflow easy to see because product, design, backend, mobile, QA, and release work all have to meet somewhere.
...
Without a board, that workflow tends to become a fragile chain of chat summaries. With Kanban, the board itself is the handoff layer. Tasks can run in parallel, wait on parent tasks, get blocked for human input, and retry with the previous run’s reason included in context.
...
and workspaces do
...
Kanban tab with task columns, task drawers, comments, event history, retry history, and recovery actions. The CLI, slash command, dashboard, and worker tools all write through the same database
...
, so the state is shared rather than copied.
...
A common mistake is to create one giant task called “Build HabitLoop”. Kanban works better when the task graph mirrors the actual project. Start with product and design, then gate engineering and QA on those outputs.
...
lets product and design feed the build instead of becoming background text lost in a chat transcript. The mobile task does not have to start before the API and screen plan exist. QA does not start until the app task has a handoff.
...
Each assignee is a Hermes profile. That profile can have its own model, memory, tools, and permissions. The official docs say Kanban workers should use the bundled`kanban-worker` skill, and the dispatcher also passes`--skills kanban-worker` when spawning workers.
...
`kanban_
...
Call`kanban
...
block as the
...
The practical value of Kanban shows up when something fails. Suppose the reviewer finds that offline check-ins can duplicate records and that a password reset token can be reused. The reviewer should block the task rather than burying the feedback in a final message.
...
The next dispatcher tick moves the task back to`ready` and starts a new run for the same assignee. The tutorial documentation notes that the new worker context includes the previous block reason, so the retry can focus on the actual problem instead of rediscovering it.

...
Now, let's walk through a complete end-to-end example to build a simple Todo List API based on FastAPI. We will strictly follow the AI-assisted TDD workflow.
...
### Step 3: The TDD Flow - Implementing the "Create Todo" Endpoint
...

...
hand this task over to Hermes Agent and have it read
...
```
# Instruct the Agent to implement the POST /todos/ route to pass the test
...
hermes --skill code_writer --args '{
  "file_path": "app/main.py",
  "prompt": "The test `test_create_todo` in `tests/test_main.py` is failing with a 404 error. Please implement the `POST /todos/` endpoint in `app/main.py`. It should: \n1. Accept a Todo item payload (without an ID). \n2. For now, use a simple in-memory list as a database. \n3. Assign a new ID to the created item. \n4. Return the complete Todo item (with ID) with a 201 Created status code. \n5. Also implement the `GET /todos/` endpoint to return the list of all todos.",
  "read_context_files": ["tests/test_main.py", "app/models.py"]
...
### Step 4: Architectural Exploration and Full-Stack Generation
...

Highlights:
### Everything you
...
Hermes Agent crossed 90,000 GitHub stars in two months. Developers are quietly building personal AI agents that learn their workflow, remember their context, and run 24/7.
...
Hermes Agent takes a fundamentally different approach which makes it much more practically useful over OpenClaw. It ships with a learning loop that:
...
This guide covers how this learning loop works, what each memory layer does, and how to configure everything from scratch.
...
By the end, you’ll have three fully isolated agents running on your machine: a programmer (who uses your Claude code), a deep researcher, and a designer, each with its own personality, memory, skills, and Telegram bot.
...
#### How it’s built
...
Everything flows through a single AIAgent class in a`run_agent.py` script. CLI, messaging gateway, batch runner, IDE integration: they’re all entry points into the same core agent.
...
The core loop is ReAct-style and synchronous. Build the system prompt, check if compression is needed, make an interruptible API call, execute any tool calls, and loop again.
...
#### Self-evolving skills
...
#### The self-improvement loop
...
This is the core differentiator. The agent creates its own skills autonomously using the`skill_manage` tool. Skill creation triggers when:
...
So the loop works like as follows
...
encounters a problem → solves it through trial and error →
...
the successful approach as a`SKILL.md` file → next time it encounters a similar problem, it loads the skill and follows the proven procedure instead of rediscovering the approach from scratch.
...

...
Evolution) solve this.

core idea is that instead of

Hermes has a first-class feature for this called profiles. Each profile is a fully isolated Hermes instance with its own config,
...
, skills, sessions, and`SOUL.md`. They share nothing by default.

result and decides what’s next.

Title: Building My First Real AI Agent with Hermes Agent - DEV Community
URL: https://dev.to/mohansri_konathala_5354a6/building-my-first-real-ai-agent-with-hermes-agent-3j5m
Published: 2026-05-29T06:14:32.000Z
Author: N/A
Highlights:
# Building My First Real AI Agent with Hermes Agent
...
### From Prompting to Autonomous Workflows
...
I wanted to understand what an actual agentic workflow feels like from a developer perspective.
...
That’s why I decided to experiment with Hermes Agent.
...
# What Made Me Interested in Hermes Agent?
...
Hermes Agent stood out because it focuses more on:
...
- open agentic workflows,
- modularity,
- developer experimentation,
- and practical integrations instead of just “chatbot experiences.”
...
# My Goal
...
So I experimented with a simple productivity-focused agent flow.
...
The idea was:
...
1. User gives a task
2. Agent breaks it down
3. Agent decides what tools to use
4. Agent processes information
5. Agent returns a structured outcome
...
# What I Learned About Agentic Systems
...
## Agents are less about intelligence and more about orchestration.
...
A strong agent workflow is really about:
...
- context handling,
- decision making,
- memory,
- execution flow,
- retries,
- and tool coordination.
...
Hermes Agent helped make that architecture easier to visualize.
...
# The Most Interesting Part: Tool Usage
...
Instead of treating the model like a chatbot, the workflow becomes:
...
- Understand task
- Decide next action
- Use appropriate tool
- Analyze result
- Continue reasoning
...
It starts feeling closer to:
...
- autonomous workflows,
- copilots,
- developer assistants,
- research agents,
- or operational systems.
...
# Challenges I Faced
...
## 1. Context management matters a lot
...
## 2. Tool chaining is harder than expected
...
## 3. “Autonomous” still needs boundaries
...
Good agent design is not about giving unlimited freedom.
...
It’s about creating:
...
- controlled execution,
- safe tool access,
- and predictable outcomes.
...
# Why Open Agentic Systems Matter
...
We’re moving from:
...
Future applications may not just:
...
- plan tasks,
- coordinate tools,
- automate workflows,
- and adapt dynamically.
...
Open systems are important because developers can:
...
- inspect behavior,
- customize flows,
- experiment safely,
- and build domain-specific agents.
...
# My Biggest Takeaway
...
After experimenting with Hermes Agent, I finally understood the difference between:
...
The future probably won’t belong to standalone models alone.
...
It will belong to ecosystems that combine:
...
- reasoning,
- tools,
- memory,
- orchestration,
- and developer control.
...
# Final Thoughts
...
This challenge pushed me to think less about prompts and more about systems.
...
- multi-agent coordination,
- memory architectures,
- retrieval systems,
- and autonomous execution pipelines.
...
But Hermes Agent made the space feel approachable enough to start experimenting seriously.
